price_list_1_date: Extract the price list issue date. Labels: 'DATE' / 'Date' / 'DATE PRICE LIST'. Examples: 'DATE: 28 FEB, 2025', 'DATE: 03/12/2025', '26.03.2025'. DO NOT confuse with valid_till. Keep the exact format from the document and add to price_list_1_date.
producer: Identify the producer/manufacturer. Sources in priority order: (1) explicit 'PRODUCER' / 'MANUFACTURER' / 'ENTERPRISE' label with company name; (2) 'Factory No' / 'ENTERPRICE NUMBER' field paired with a producer name; (3) FALLBACK: when no explicit producer label exists, use the company name from the document HEADER (logo + company name at the top). Examples: 'Akerko Su Urunleri', 'TIME SEAFOOD (DALIAN) CO., LTD'. Keep the full name (and address if shown) in one line. Add to producer.
seller: Identify the seller. Labels: 'Seller' / 'Supplier'. Most price lists do NOT show an explicit 'Seller' label — in that case use the company name from the document HEADER (the issuing company). If neither label nor identifiable header company is present, leave empty. Add to seller.
incoterms: Extract Incoterms — three-letter code (EXW/FOB/CFR/CIF/DAP, etc.) and, if shown, the place. Labels: 'Term of shipment' / 'TERM OF DELIVERY' / 'TERMS OF DELIVERY'. Examples: 'EXW RONGCHENG', 'FOB QINGDAO', 'CFR SAINT-PETERSBURG (INCOTERMS 2010)', 'DAP MOSCOW'. Keep wording exactly as printed. Add to incoterms.
valid_till: Extract validity period end. Labels: 'VALID TILL' / 'VALID UNTIL' / 'Valid until'. Examples: 'VALID TILL: 15 APR, 2025', '30/04/2026'. DO NOT confuse with price_list_1_date (issue date). Keep the value as-is and add to valid_till.
HS_code: Extract HS/tariff code(s). Labels: 'HS CODE' / 'HS Code' / 'Tariff code'. Format may vary: 6 digits with dot ('0304.81'), 10 digits without separators ('0303899007', '2001909709'). If multiple, join with '; '. If no code, leave empty. Add to HS_code.
country_of_origin: Extract origin country. Labels: 'ORIGIN OF GOODS' / 'Country of origin' / 'Origin' / 'ORIGIN'. May appear as a column header in the goods table or as a separate field. Examples: 'CHILE', 'CHINA'. If absent, leave empty. Add to country_of_origin.
products: The 'products' block is expandable: create product_1, product_2, ... for each line item in the price list, keeping order. For each position fill all available fields; if a shared attribute (e.g., one Latin species for multiple positions) is given once, duplicate it in the relevant product_i. In token_refs include references to the source fragments (cells/rows) used.
products[*].name_product: Extract the commercial name. Labels: 'PRODUCT' / 'Description' / 'DESCRIPTION OF PRODUCTS' / 'DESCRIPTION OF GOODS'. Examples: 'FROZEN OILFISH HGT', 'FROZEN RAINBOW TROUT WR', 'FROZEN SEAWEED SALAD (CHUKA SALAD)'. Join line breaks while keeping exact spelling. Add to products[i].name_product.
products[*].latin_name: Extract the Latin species name. Sources: (1) most often in parentheses after the commercial name (e.g., '(LEPIDOCYBIUM FLAVOBRUNNEUM)', '(UNDARIA PINNATIFIDA)', '(Oncorhynchus mykiss)'); (2) less commonly in a separate field 'LATIN NAME:' (e.g., 'LATIN NAME: ONCORHYNCHUS MYKISS'). Note: not every parenthesized text is Latin — pick only the scientific species name. If absent, leave empty. Add to products[i].latin_name.
products[*].size_product: Extract size/grade. Examples: '1-3KG', '3-6KG', '6-10KG', '10KG+', '0.8-1.2 KG', '6-9 LB (2,7-4,0 KG)', '1KG/BAG*10/CARTON'. Keep both systems if both are printed. Add to products[i].size_product.
products[*].unit_box: Extract the pricing unit if explicitly shown. Examples: 'BOX', 'BAG', 'CARTON', 'BAGS', 'KG', 'UNIT'. Often part of the price column header (e.g., 'Unit Price RMB/BAGS', 'PRICE USD/CARTON BOX'). Keep the wording as in the document. Add to products[i].unit_box.
products[*].packages: Extract package quantity if a packaging column is present. Labels: 'Packages' / 'Cartons' / 'Cases' / 'KG/BOX'. Most price lists do NOT include package counts — if no such field, leave empty. Add to products[i].packages.
products[*].price_per_unit: Extract the unit price (PER PACKAGE/UNIT, distinguishing from PRICE_LIST_2 which is per kilogram). Labels: 'Price' / 'Price per CARTON' / 'Unit Price RMB/BAGS' / 'PRICE USD/CARTON BOX' / 'Цена за коробку'. Examples: 'RMB 569.692/bag', 'CNY 90.00/CARTON', 'USD 111.656084/CARTON', 'USD 175.00/BOX'. Keep currency and number format (dot/comma) exactly as in the document. Add to products[i].price_per_unit.
products[*].total_price: Extract line total if a total column exists. Labels: 'Total' / 'Line total' / 'Ext. price' / 'Сумма'. Most price lists do NOT have line totals — if absent, leave empty (do not compute packages × price_per_unit). Add to products[i].total_price.
